raw_consumer_statement:
I bought a desktop computer along with its cables from Best Buy online on XXXX XXXX, 2023, for a total of {$130.00}. The items were delivered to me on XXXX XXXX, 2023, and I received the receipt confirming everything in order was correct and accurate. Upon receiving my monthly statement on XX/XX/XXXX, I noticed that the total amount was {$640.00}, which was inaccurate. I was able to identify the below items as fraudulent. 
XX/XX/XXXX BESTBUY.COM, XXXXBESTBUY {$400.00} XX/XX/XXXX BESTBUY.COM, XXXXBESTBUY {$98.00}. 
I contacted the customer support team at BestBuy to report that my credit card had been compromised. I informed them that there were XXXX items appearing on my statement that I did not purchase and did not recognize. They assured me that they would investigate the matter right away. They closed the old credit card and issued another new credit card. However, to my dismay, after XXXX days, on XX/XX/XXXX, I received an email stating that I had failed to properly identify the items, resulting in the dismissal of my case. I was left wondering how it was possible I did not identify and I m the one who called customer service to report an unrecognized purchase in my billing account. This information is both incorrect and misleading for Citi Bank/BestBuy credit cards . I pointed out the fraudulent items, providing specific dates of the suspicious activity, for them to conduct a thorough investigation. Additionally, I emphasized to the BestBuy/ Citi bank XXXX XXXX team that my BestBuy purchase order list account summary did not have these fraudulent items, which are listed on my credit card statement. Despite presenting ample evidence, they closed my case and demanded full payment. After I filed my case with the Office of Attorney General XXXX they opened the investigation, they requested a police report whereby I reported my case to XXXX police station on XXXX XXXX. Therefore, I am seeking assistance in resolving this matter.
